claim: The largest AI opportunity lies in replacing services with software, not just replacing software with software; TAM could be in the trillions compared to cloud's hundreds of billions
actionable: When evaluating AI business models, distinguish between automation (replacing worker roles) and augmentation (enhancing worker capability); focus on high-value service markets (legal, customer support, medical) for maximum TAM
source_title: The AI opportunity: Sequoia Capital's AI Ascent 2024 opening remarks

claim: Three core LLM capabilities enable business applications: create (images, text, audio), reason (one-shot and multi-step), and interact humanlike; having both create and reason means software can now interact like humans
actionable: When designing AI features, map requirements to one of three capability buckets (create/reason/interact); ensure your use case actually requires multi-step reasoning or humanlike interaction, not just generation
source_title: The AI opportunity: Sequoia Capital's AI Ascent 2024 opening remarks

claim: Current AI apps have poor retention metrics compared to mobile: daily-to-monthly active user ratios and one-month retention fall far short of mobile app peers, indicating AI remains infrequent use (once weekly/monthly) rather than daily habit
actionable: Before scaling an AI product, analyze your DAU/MAU ratio and monthly retention against mobile benchmarks; if below mobile standards, focus on increasing usage frequency and habit formation before acquisition
source_title: The AI opportunity: Sequoia Capital's AI Ascent 2024 opening remarks

claim: The expectations-reality gap drives AI app churn; users see impressive Twitter demos but models aren't reliable enough for real tasks, gap narrows as base models improve but still significant blocker to adoption
actionable: Test AI features heavily with target users before public release; measure and report actual reliability metrics in your product, not cherry-picked demo results; build in graceful degradation when models fail
source_title: The AI opportunity: Sequoia Capital's AI Ascent 2024 opening remarks

claim: Customer support automation is achieving product-market fit early: Clara using OpenAI has automated equivalent of 700 FTE agents; this is the rule, not the exception, for customer service AI
actionable: If building customer support AI, benchmark against Clara's metrics (2/3 of inquiries handled); validate product-market fit by measuring cost-per-inquiry and first-contact resolution rate
source_title: The AI opportunity: Sequoia Capital's AI Ascent 2024 opening remarks

claim: AI agents that take humans out of the loop entirely (not co-pilots) will be the major shift in 2024-2025; visible in software engineering, customer service, and knowledge work
actionable: Shift from co-pilot UI (suggestions on the side) to agent orchestration UIs; design for autonomous task completion with human oversight rather than assistance-based interaction patterns
source_title: The AI opportunity: Sequoia Capital's AI Ascent 2024 opening remarks

claim: Test-time compute and inference-time thinking will be a major research thrust; giving models time to think through planning and reasoning improves performance on complex tasks more than just scaling model size
actionable: For complex reasoning tasks, implement multi-step prompts with intermediate verification steps rather than expecting single-pass answers; measure latency vs. accuracy tradeoffs for your use case
source_title: The AI opportunity: Sequoia Capital's AI Ascent 2024 opening remarks

claim: The gap between GPU spending ($50B) and AI revenue generated ($3B) is massive and unsustainable; companies must find ways to generate value more efficiently or spend will collapse
actionable: Monitor unit economics of AI services; if acquiring users costs more than lifetime value times gross margin, focus on efficiency improvements (prompt optimization, smaller models, inference caching) before scaling spend
source_title: The AI opportunity: Sequoia Capital's AI Ascent 2024 opening remarks

claim: Shift from pre-training compute to inference compute is underway; inference latency and cost matter more as applications move to production, marking the end of pre-training-dominated economics
actionable: In 2024-2025, prioritize inference optimization (quantization, caching, distillation) over larger models; measure cost-per-inference and latency SLAs for production workloads; consider smaller models for edge cases
source_title: The AI opportunity: Sequoia Capital's AI Ascent 2024 opening remarks

claim: Future of software is agents working together in networks (multi-agent systems), not single agents; analogy: from single reaper to combined harvester with many coordinated machine systems
actionable: Design AI systems with inter-agent communication and task delegation; implement message passing between agents and explicit handoff mechanisms rather than monolithic single-agent orchestration
source_title: The AI opportunity: Sequoia Capital's AI Ascent 2024 opening remarks

claim: AI will enable cost reduction in education, healthcare, and housing by making these services cheaper; productivity revolutions historically have been deflationary (software costs down, TV prices down)
actionable: When building AI for high-cost services (legal, medical, education), model economics around cost reduction rather than new revenue; margin expansion comes from efficiency, not upselling
source_title: The AI opportunity: Sequoia Capital's AI Ascent 2024 opening remarks
